ogTitle: Fawcetts Plain vs Fennell Bay: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Fawcetts Plain and Fennell Bay.
heading: Fawcetts Plain vs Fennell Bay — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Fawcetts Plain, New South Wales and Fennell Bay, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Fawcetts Plain has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to Fennell Bay (45/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Fawcetts Plain or Fennell Bay safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Fawcetts Plain has a flood risk score of 44/100, while Fennell Bay scores 58/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Fawcetts Plain scores 17/100 for bushfire risk. Fennell Bay scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Fawcetts Plain or Fennell Bay?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Fawcetts Plain has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.17% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
